concerns.text: Task T028a (Simulation Loop) is marked as incomplete (unchecked) but is a hard prerequisite for T029 (Aggregate Metrics), T030 (Visualization), T039 (Comparison Report), and T053 (Mixed-Effects). The plan implies T028a must run to produce `results/simulation_results.csv` before any aggregation or analysis can occur. The current order leaves the entire US3/US4 analysis pipeline blocked by an unstarted foundational task.

Task T029 (calculate_aggregate_metrics) depends on T028c (Result Aggregation & CSV Writing), which in turn depends on T028a. T028a is unchecked. T029 is also unchecked. This creates a dependency chain where the core metric calculation (FR-005) cannot proceed because the data generation loop (T028a) is not marked as a prerequisite completion point in the task list, despite the text explicitly stating 'Dependency: Requires T028c'.

Task T039 (Comparison Report) depends on T038 (Real-World Results) AND T029 (Synthetic Aggregates). T029 is unchecked and blocked by T028a. T038 is unchecked and blocked by T056. The task list does not enforce the sequential completion of the synthetic data generation (T028a -> T029) before attempting the comparison report (T039), risking a 'dead end' where the comparison logic is implemented but has no input data.

Task T053 (Mixed-Effects Model) depends on T028c (Synthetic Results) and T056 (Real-World Data). T028c is unchecked and blocked by T028a. T056 is unchecked. The ordering allows T053 to be considered 'in progress' while its primary data sources (T028a/T028c) are explicitly marked as incomplete, violating the 'Producer before Consumer' rule for the simulation results artifact.

Task T028a is listed as incomplete, yet T063, T064, and T065 (which it depends on) are marked complete. The visual ordering suggests T028a is the next logical step, but the unchecked status combined with the unchecked status of all downstream consumers (T029, T030, T039, T053) creates a semantic blockage. The task list should explicitly group T028a as a 'Critical Path' blocker or ensure the dependency arrows are visually reinforced in the text description.
